fields.description:
## Context

Rebar is a reusable ticket, reconciliation, and review-gate system consumed through several harnesses and deployed into projects with very different stacks, repository shapes, platforms, and execution environments. Development of rebar itself happens primarily in a Python repository on macOS through current agent harnesses, which creates a risk that a technically plausible plan silently bakes rebar-project assumptions into reusable behavior.

Representative failures include a core `rebar landing` command that encodes this repository Gerrit policy, Python-only review rules that fail to apply to a Next.js client, macOS-specific dependencies, and operations that assume the process starts inside a local project checkout. The review must catch concrete incompatibilities without requiring every plan to contain generic portability prose.

## Why

Rebar maintainers need an early, high-recall warning when a proposed plan would break a supported client shape. Downstream clients need project-specific policy to remain in project configuration while reusable mechanisms remain usable across harnesses, target projects, platforms, execution venues, and project-location models. Dogfooding the existing project-criterion and project-coaching seams also provides a real example for client authors.

## What

Add one activated project plan-review criterion, `project.portability`, through `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` and `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`. It runs in Pass 1 at both container and leaf scope. It does not adjudicate validity, impact, severity, or disposition; existing Pass 2 and Pass 3 do that without modification.

The finder may emit a portability finding only when it supplies all four elements:

- a citation to the explicit plan detail or mechanism carrying the assumption;
- a materially different supported client shape;
- the causal mechanism by which that shape fails; and
- a specific, observable breakage scenario that Pass 2 can verify.

The compatibility counterexample matrix is:

- Harness: Python library, CLI, and remote MCP clients, without assumptions specific to Claude Code, Codex, or another development harness.
- Target project: Ruby, Python, Java, Next.js, .NET, and atypical layouts such as Terraform subprojects inside a monorepo.
- Platform and venue: macOS, Windows, Linux, and BSD across CI, servers, and developer workstations.
- Project location and access: in-checkout current working directory, explicitly located workspace, and server execution outside the target checkout without assuming unrestricted local filesystem access.

Silence about portability is not a finding. If the reviewer cannot construct the cited, concrete counterexample, it emits no finding. Project-specific behavior is allowed when it remains project configuration rather than leaking into reusable core behavior.

Add one project-specific Pass-4 move in `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`, applicable only when `project.portability` survives review:

- id: `project-portability`
- name: `restore rebar portability`
- applies_when: `["project.portability"]`
- template: `Rework {subject} so it remains portable across supported rebar client shapes; keep project-specific behavior in project configuration or an explicit extension boundary.`

The existing Pass-4 applicability filter, move picker, subject validator, and deterministic renderer remain unchanged. No generic core coaching move is added.

## Approach and sequencing

The fresh-worktree experiment is complete and recorded on this epic. It mechanically proved criterion activation for leaf and container plans, `ProductionBatchRunner` project fan-in, project move loading, criterion-scoped applicability, and deterministic rendering against current `origin/main`. The temporary experiment files were removed; implementation remains outstanding.

Delivery is decomposed into four bounded children:

1. `immune-floury-toad` activates and integrates the Pass 1 finder.
2. After the finder lands, `truthful-specified-silverfish` adds project-scoped Pass 4 coaching while `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` independently calibrates the live criterion against balanced positive and negative cases.
3. After coaching and calibration land, `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` documents the final project extension pattern and runs repository-wide regression gates.

This approach uses the existing project extension seams instead of changing the generic four-pass kernel. It directly demonstrates the intended boundary, gives live semantics a separate calibration gate, limits blast radius, and preserves Pass 2 and Pass 3 behavior.

## Child delivery map

- `immune-floury-toad` — owns routing, activation, prompt resolution, leaf/container fan-in, rubric structure, false-positive instructions, and overlay-absent integration coverage.
- `truthful-specified-silverfish` — owns only the `project-portability` move, its criterion-scoped applicability, deterministic rendering, and proof that no generic core move changes.
- `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` — owns the balanced live eval corpus, four-part positive findings, must-not-fire boundaries, repeat-run thresholds, and any calibration-driven edits confined to the project rubric.
- `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` — owns the client-facing dogfood documentation, parser-safe placement outside the built-in move table, and final lint/typecheck/test verification.
- The completed worktree experiment remains parent-level operator evidence because it de-risks the whole decomposition and is not remaining implementation work.

The dependency graph is `immune-floury-toad -> {truthful-specified-silverfish, indigoid-unmystic-serpent} -> taurine-catchable-kakarikis`.

## Scope

In scope:

- Rebar-project Pass-1 criterion configuration, prompt, and activation.
- Rebar-project Pass-4 move configuration restricted to `project.portability`.
- Offline wiring experiment in a fresh worktree.
- Focused positive, negative, leaf, container, routing, and rendering tests.
- A balanced live evaluation corpus with repeat-run release thresholds.
- Documentation of the project configuration as an example for clients.

Out of scope:

- Remediating existing portability debt discovered by the criterion.
- Adding `rebar landing` or other rebar-project workflow policy to reusable core.
- A generic built-in coaching move.
- Changes to Pass 2 validity/impact adjudication or Pass 3 deterministic decisions.
- Guaranteeing that every existing rebar feature already supports every matrix cell.

## Success Criteria

- The rebar project owns and activates `project.portability`, and the production Pass-1 routing includes it for both container and leaf plans.
- The project move loads through the production registry, is offered only for findings carrying `project.portability`, and renders the locked project template.
- A balanced three-run evaluation demonstrates all four concrete portability failures while producing no finding for silence or valid project scoping.
- Existing plan-review behavior remains unchanged when a client has no project overlay or project move file.
- The experiment evidence and implementation documentation show client authors how the two project extension seams compose.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `immune-floury-toad` activates `project.portability` with `exec: 1-TURN`, `facet: project-invariants`, `applies_at.scope: [container, leaf]`, `default_posture: blocking`, and `block_threshold: 0.9`. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k criterion_activation`
- [ ] `immune-floury-toad` proves the contract-bearing project rubric resolves through the production prompt loader. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k prompt_resolution`
- [ ] `immune-floury-toad` proves both leaf and container plans route through production project fan-in. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k "leaf_routing or container_routing or production_fan_in"`
- [ ] `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` proves every must-fire case defines a gold four-part expected counterexample: plan citation, alternate client shape, causal mechanism, plus observable breakage. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k finding_contract`
- [ ] `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` proves a plan with no portability discussion is a must-not-fire case. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k negative_boundaries`
- [ ] `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` proves specialization behind project configuration or an explicit extension boundary is a must-not-fire case. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k negative_boundaries`
- [ ] [operator-attested] `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` records three live eval runs with recall `1.0`, false-accept rate `0.0`, per-case stability of at least two of three, and Cohen kappa of at least `0.70`. Verify: `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json`
- [ ] `truthful-specified-silverfish` proves the `project-portability` move applies only when `project.portability` survives review. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_coaching.py -k applies_to_portability -k ignores_unrelated_findings`
- [ ] `truthful-specified-silverfish` proves the move deterministically renders the locked project template and does not add a generic core move. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_coaching.py -k "deterministic_render or project_owned_only"`
- [ ] [operator-attested] The completed fresh-`origin/main` worktree experiment proves criterion activation through Pass-1 fan-in plus project-move applicability/rendering. Evidence on this epic names the worktree, branch, base SHA, commands, assertions, output, and timestamp.
- [ ] `immune-floury-toad` proves overlay-absent plan-review behavior remains green. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py`
- [ ] `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` documents the exact project criterion and project move composition outside the built-in move table. Verify: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py`
- [ ] `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` proves repository lint, type checks, and tests pass. Verify: `make lint && make typecheck && make test`
